request.query: mutation CreateMemberQuery($input: CustomerSegmentMembersQueryInput!) { customerSegmentMembersQueryCreate(input: $input) { customerSegmentMembersQuery { id currentCount done } userErrors { field message } } }

mutation CreateMemberQuery($input: CustomerSegmentMembersQueryInput!) { customerSegmentMembersQueryCreate(input: $input) { customerSegmentMembersQuery { id currentCount done } userErrors { field message } } }
query Lookup($id: ID!) { customerSegmentMembersQuery(id: $id) { id currentCount done } }

query Members($queryId: ID!, $first: Int) { customerSegmentMembers(queryId: $queryId, first: $first) { totalCount statistics { amountSpent: attributeStatistics(attributeName: "amount_spent") { average sum } } edges { cursor node { id } } pageInfo { hasNextPage hasPreviousPage startCursor endCursor } } }
query Membership($customerId: ID!, $segmentIds: [ID!]!) { customerSegmentMembership(customerId: $customerId, segmentIds: $segmentIds) { memberships { segmentId isMember } } }

notes: A separate live setup/cleanup run created three disposable tagged customers and a segment on harry-test-heelo.myshopify.com, captured query-job polling to done=true, two-page member pagination with totalCount=3, segmentId member reads, membership isMember=true, then deleted the segment and customers.
Local parity keeps the executable generic scenario to the empty numeric query branch; runtime integration tests cover staged positive pagination and membership with deterministic local IDs.
